name: Moriscos - Huerta de Ricote Trail
name_original: Senda de los Moriscos - Umbría de Ricote (Circular PR-MU 8)
region: Zepa Sierra De Ricote Y La Navela
description: Hike the moderate Moriscos - Huerta de Ricote Trail for 4.5 miles (7.3 km) through orchards, historic towns, and scenic viewpoints.
region_intro: Family friendly hiking trails in Zepa Sierra De Ricote Y La Navela traverse a diverse landscape, characterized by rugged mountainous terrain and lush riverine environments along the Segura River. The region features Aleppo pine forests, kermes oak, and unique geological formations. Designated as a Special Area of Conservation (ZEC) and a Special Protection Area for Birds (ZEPA), it offers varied natural settings for outdoor activities. Best family friendly hiking trails in Zepa Sierra De Ricote Y La Navela The most popular family friendly hiking route is Scenic Valley View loop from Ulea, a 3.6 miles (5.9 km) trail that takes 1 hour 35 minutes to complete. This easy route offers views of the surrounding valley. Another top favourite among local hikers is Mirador del Solvente loop from Ojós, a moderate 6.8 miles (11.0 km) path. This route includes significant elevation gain and panoramic views from Mirador del Solvente. Local hikers also love the Azud de Ojós Reservoir – Azud de Ojós Reservoir loop from Blanca, a 5.8 miles (9.4 km) trail leading through the riverine environment, often completed in about 2 hours 29 minutes. Hiking in Zepa Sierra De Ricote Y La Navela is defined by mountainous terrain, river valleys, and pine forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, including 21 easy and 44 moderate routes. The routes in Zepa Sierra De Ricote Y La Navela are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 240 reviews. More than 1300 hikers have used komoot to explore Zepa Sierra De Ricote Y La Navela's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many family-friendly hiking trails are available in Zepa Sierra De Ricote Y La Navela?
A: Zepa Sierra De Ricote Y La Navela offers a wide selection of routes suitable for families, with over 70 hiking trails available. Among these, you'll find more than 20 easy routes, perfect for a relaxed outing with children, and over 40 moderate options for families seeking a bit more adventure.

Q: What kind of terrain can we expect on family hikes in Zepa Sierra De Ricote Y La Navela?
A: The region features a diverse landscape, from rugged mountainous terrain with Aleppo pine forests to lush riverine environments along the Segura River. Family-friendly trails often follow the fertile plains and riverbanks, offering gentle paths, while some moderate routes might include slight elevation changes with rewarding panoramic views. You'll encounter a mix of natural paths and sometimes paved sections, especially near towns.

Q: Are there any easy circular routes suitable for families with young children?
A: Yes, there are several easy circular routes perfect for families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/27629795" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Scenic Valley View loop from Ulea&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 5.8 km trail that offers lovely views without significant climbs. Another great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17270668" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ricote Valley loop from Villanueva del Río Segura&lt;/a&gt;, which is just over 5.5 km and very gentle, ideal for a leisurely family walk.

Q: What are some interesting natural attractions or viewpoints to see along the trails?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty and offers several stunning viewpoints. You could visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5445526" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador del Solvente&lt;/a&gt;, which provides views of Ulea Castle and the Ojós orchard. Another notable spot is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3044804" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Azud de Ojós and Ricote from the Senda de los Moriscos&lt;/a&gt;, offering picturesque scenes of the Azud de Ojós and the town of Ricote. For a more challenging but rewarding view, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4827909" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almeces&lt;/a&gt; peak offers spectacular panoramas.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the family-friendly hiking trails in Zepa Sierra De Ricote Y La Navela?
A: Many trails in Zepa Sierra De Ricote Y La Navela are dog-friendly, allowing you to bring your furry companions along. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in protected areas like this ZEPA (Special Protection Area for Birds), to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers. Please also remember to clean up after your pet.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking with family in this region?
A: The best time for family hiking in Zepa Sierra De Ricote Y La Navela is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) months. During these seasons, the weather is milder and more pleasant, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, sometimes wetter, conditions of winter. Spring also brings blooming wildflowers, while autumn offers beautiful foliage.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by water features or reservoirs?
A: Yes, the region's proximity to the Segura River means several trails incorporate water features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38236880" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Azud de Ojós Reservoir – Azud de Ojós Reservoir loop from Blanca&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 9.3 km route that takes you around the reservoir, offering lovely views of the water. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40356359" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jarral River Beach – Noria Grande loop from Blanca&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 13.2 km trail that explores riverine environments.

Q: What do other hikers say about the family-friendly trails in Zepa Sierra De Ricote Y La Navela?
A: The komoot community highly rates the hiking experience in Zepa Sierra De Ricote Y La Navela, with an average score of 4.4 out of 5 stars from over 240 ratings. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, the well-maintained paths, and the stunning panoramic views, making it a popular choice for families looking to explore nature together.

Q: Are there any historical sites or ruins that can be visited on a family hike?
A: Yes, the region has a rich history. While not directly on a family-friendly route listed here, the Castle of Ricote is a notable historical site. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1409168" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado de la Madera Trail&lt;/a&gt;, though longer, is described as a 'mythical trail' of the Sierra de Ricote and offers spectacular views, hinting at the historical significance of the area. You might also encounter ancient agricultural structures or remnants of past settlements along various paths.

Q: Is parking readily available near the trailheads for family hikes?
A: Many trailheads in the towns and villages surrounding Zepa Sierra De Ricote Y La Navela, such as Ulea, Ojós, Blanca, and Villanueva del Río Segura, offer convenient parking options. It's generally easy to find places to park your car before starting your family adventure, especially in designated areas near popular starting points. Always check local signage for any specific parking regulations.

Q: Are there any trails suitable for strollers or very young children?
A: While many trails are natural paths, some sections, particularly those along the river or within village limits, might be suitable for sturdy strollers. For very young children, the easier, shorter loops like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17270668" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ricote Valley loop from Villanueva del Río Segura&lt;/a&gt; are ideal, as they have minimal elevation gain and are relatively flat. Always check the route details and terrain description on komoot before heading out to ensure it meets your family's needs.
tips: Panoramic point on the Senda de los Moriscos with beautiful views of the Azud de Ojós and the town of Ricote, surrounded by a very picturesque landscape.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The Moriscos - Huerta de Ricote Trail is approximately 7.3 km (4.5 miles) long. Most hikers complete the route in about 2 hours to 3 hours 30 minutes, depending on their pace and how often they stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the route?
faqs.answer: This trail is generally considered to be of easy to medium difficulty, making it suitable for a wide range of hikers, including families. While mostly accessible, the climb to the "Ventanica de Ojós" arch is noted as a slightly more challenging section.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The trail typically starts and ends in the charming old town of Ricote. You can find parking options within the town, which provides convenient access to the route.

faqs.question: Is the Moriscos - Huerta de Ricote Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through populated areas, agricultural zones, or near wildlife. Please also ensure you clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: Spring (March to May) is ideal, as the "Huerta de Ricote" is vibrant, and you can enjoy the fragrant orange blossoms. Autumn (September to November) also offers pleasant temperatures and beautiful colors. Summer can be very hot in the Region of Murcia, so if hiking then, start early, carry plenty of water, and be prepared for high temperatures. Winter hiking is also possible, offering cooler, crisp conditions.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers a diverse landscape, including regular paths, forest tracks, and ancient irrigation channels (acequias). You'll encounter sections with a unique paved surface and a narrow stone corridor known as "El Carrerón." The route moves between the old town of Ricote, mountainous sections, and the fertile orchards of the "Huerta de Ricote."

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll discover a rich blend of natural beauty and historical sites. Highlights include panoramic views of the Ricote Valley, the Segura River, and the Ojós reservoir. Don't miss the View of the Azud de Ojós and Ricote from the Senda de los Moriscos, the "Ventanica de Ojós" natural arch, and the lush "Huerta de Ricote" with its fruit and palm trees. The trail also passes through Ricote's medieval old town, historic buildings like the Parish Church of San Sebastián, and remnants of the 16th-century Ermita de Nuestra Señora de Las Huertas.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to hike in the Valle de Ricote?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the trails within the Valle de Ricote, including the Moriscos - Huerta de Ricote Trail. However, always respect local regulations, especially concerning private property, agricultural areas, and natural protected zones.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails in the area?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other paths. You'll find yourself on parts of the Senda de los Moriscos - Huerta de Ricote, Vereda de Ojós, and the Senda El Carrerón, among others, which contribute to its varied and interesting course.

faqs.question: Is there water available along the trail?
faqs.answer: While the trail passes through the town of Ricote where you can find amenities, there might be a lack of natural water sources directly along the route, especially during drier seasons. It's highly recommended to carry sufficient water for your entire hike, particularly on warmer days.

faqs.question: Is the trail a circular route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Moriscos - Huerta de Ricote Trail is a circular path, meaning you will end up back at your starting point in Ricote without having to retrace your steps.

waypoints.name: View of the Azud de Ojós and Ricote from the Senda de los Moriscos
